title: coelopleurum saxatile
intro: Coelopleurum saxatile, also known as Korean angelica, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used in Korean medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving circulation, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer and antiviral effects. Coelopleurum saxatile can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
